mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, reducing default risk and supporting corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive 2-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side constraints and manufacturing contraction, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances during inflationary expansions, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window or positive return frequency falls below 52% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month inflation rate exceeding 5.0% signals persistent price pressures that force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption sectors, reducing recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth condition fails to yield a positive return frequency above 57% over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing weakness and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases, or the predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) when tested exclusively in late-cycle regimes.
